# =============================================================================
# Seq — Music Thing Workshop System drum machine
# Milestone 0: passthrough baseline build
#
# Goal of this file: produce a Seq.uf2 that flashes cleanly, survives a power
# cycle and reset, and has all the platform-correct knobs already turned —
# 16 MB flash, 144 MHz core clock, code-in-RAM, USB-stdio off, crash-on-reset
# workaround in place. Every later milestone inherits these defaults.
# =============================================================================

# == DO NOT EDIT THE FOLLOWING LINES for the Raspberry Pi Pico VS Code Extension to work ==
if(WIN32)
    set(USERHOME $ENV{USERPROFILE})
else()
    set(USERHOME $ENV{HOME})
endif()
set(sdkVersion 2.2.0)
set(toolchainVersion 14_2_Rel1)
set(picotoolVersion 2.2.0)
set(picoVscode ${USERHOME}/.pico-sdk/cmake/pico-vscode.cmake)
if (EXISTS ${picoVscode})
    include(${picoVscode})
endif()
# ====================================================================================

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.13)

set(PICO_BOARD pico CACHE STRING "Board type")

# --- 16 MB flash card ---------------------------------------------------------
# 16 MiB = 16 * 1024 * 1024 = 16777216 bytes.
# Setting this BEFORE pico_sdk_init() is what tells the SDK to lay out the
# linker script for a 16 MB device. Skipping this works on first flash but
# can corrupt later when code/data crosses the 2 MB default boundary.
set(PICO_FLASH_SIZE_BYTES 16777216)

include(pico_sdk_import.cmake)
project(Seq C CXX ASM)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
pico_sdk_init()

# --- Sources ------------------------------------------------------------------
# M0 has only main.cpp. Later milestones append: voice.cpp, sequencer.cpp,
# humanize.cpp, euclidean.cpp, ui.cpp.
add_executable(Seq
    main.cpp
)

# --- Compiler hygiene ---------------------------------------------------------
# -Wdouble-promotion catches accidental promotion of float to double.
# Doubles on RP2040 are software-emulated and ~10× slower than float; one
# stray sin() instead of sinf() can blow the per-sample budget.
target_compile_options(Seq PRIVATE
    -Wdouble-promotion
    -Wfloat-conversion
    -Wall
    -Wextra
)

# Print RAM/flash usage at link time so we always see how much budget remains.
target_link_options(Seq PRIVATE -Wl,--print-memory-usage)

# --- ESSENTIAL: oscillator startup workaround ---------------------------------
# Without this, the firmware runs fine on the first flash but FAILS after a
# reset (XOSC needs more time to stabilise on the Workshop Computer than the
# SDK default allows). This catches roughly half of all reported "it doesn't
# boot" issues from new card developers.
target_compile_definitions(Seq PRIVATE PICO_XOSC_STARTUP_DELAY_MULTIPLIER=64)

target_include_directories(Seq PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR})

# --- Libraries ----------------------------------------------------------------
# pico_multicore is pulled in now (even though M0 single-cores) so adding the
# sequencer + humanizer to core 1 later doesn't require a CMake edit.
target_link_libraries(Seq
    pico_stdlib
    pico_multicore
    pico_unique_id
    hardware_dma
    hardware_i2c
    hardware_pwm
    hardware_adc
    hardware_spi
    hardware_clocks
    hardware_flash
)

# --- Code in RAM --------------------------------------------------------------
# Eliminates flash-cache-miss timing jitter inside the audio ISR. We have
# 264 KB of RAM and Phase-1 Seq is well under that, so there's no reason not
# to do this. (Drumdrum, sample_and_hold, sine_wave_lookup all do the same.)
pico_set_binary_type(Seq copy_to_ram)

pico_add_extra_outputs(Seq)

# --- USB and UART stdio OFF ---------------------------------------------------
# USB stdio interferes with the normalisation probe and the ADC. UART stdio
# would steal GPIO4 (which IS the normalisation probe). Use SWD for debug.
pico_enable_stdio_usb(Seq 0)
pico_enable_stdio_uart(Seq 0)

# --- Convenience: copy UF2 next to the sources --------------------------------
# Saves digging into build/ every time you want to drag-and-drop the binary
# onto the Workshop Computer's USB drive.
add_custom_command(TARGET Seq POST_BUILD
    COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
        "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:Seq>/Seq.uf2"
        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/Seq.uf2"
    COMMENT "Copying Seq.uf2 to source directory")
